item_quest_tables.note: Each rarity tier has independent pools for hook, origin, quest_type, steps, reward_hint, and oracle_seed. A quest is assembled by picking one entry from each pool at random. Steps pools contain arrays of possible step sequences — pick one full array per quest.

uncommon.description: Simple mysteries or small obligations. One-session stories.
hooks: There is a name scratched into the hilt — someone cared enough to mark it.
The object is warm to the touch even in the coldest room.
A merchant recognized it and went pale before walking away quickly.
It was found on a body with no other belongings — as if left there deliberately.
A child in the last town pointed at it and said 'that belongs to grandmother'.
The object smells faintly of a place you have never been.
It still has someone else's blood on it. Old blood. Not from combat.
A symbol on it matches one you saw on a wanted notice.
It was sealed in a locked box with no key and no explanation.
Someone left a note with it: 'Do not use this until you understand what it costs'.
It hums softly at night when you are certain no one else is awake.
The previous owner sold it in a hurry, for far less than it was worth.
It was buried. Not lost — deliberately buried.
A priest of a forgotten god blessed it. The blessing is still active.
Someone has been asking about it in every town you have passed through.
origins: Crafted by a journeyman artificer who disappeared before finishing their masterwork.
Belonged to a soldier who never came home from a battle fought three decades ago.
Made as a wedding gift that was never delivered.
Commissioned by a merchant guild as a symbol of rank — then the guild dissolved.
Created in a small village as a protective charm; the village no longer exists.
Won in a card game by someone who did not know its value.
Left behind by a traveler who passed through and never returned.
Part of a matching set; the other pieces are unaccounted for.
Made by a craftsperson to pay off a debt they could not settle in coin.
Taken from a shrine as an offering that was never meant to leave.
Inherited through a family line that has now ended.
Recovered from a shipwreck along with nothing else of value.
Traded between hands so many times its original owner is unknown.
Created during a festival that only happens once a generation.
Found in the rubble of a building that burned down under suspicious circumstances.
steps_pools: Find out who the name on the object belongs to.
Locate that person or their family.
Return the object and learn why it matters.
Ask the Oracle who is searching for this object right now.
Decide whether to keep it, sell it, or seek out the searcher.
Follow the lead wherever it takes you.
Trace the object back to its last known owner.
Discover what happened to them.
Determine whether the object played a role.
Find someone who recognizes the symbol or marking on the object.
Ask them what they know.
Decide whether the truth changes anything for you.
Use the Oracle to determine whether the object is wanted by someone dangerous.
If yes — decide whether to run, fight, or negotiate.
Resolve the situation on your terms.
reward_hints: The gratitude of a family that had given up hope.
A piece of information worth more than the object itself.
The respect of someone who has been watching you.
A small debt repaid by the universe — something goes your way soon.
Peace of mind, and perhaps a clearer conscience.
A contact in an unexpected place.
The satisfaction of finishing something someone else left undone.
A rumor that leads somewhere more interesting.
oracle_seeds: Is someone in the nearest settlement looking for this object right now?
Did the previous owner give it up willingly?
Does this object have a reputation in this region?
Is there danger attached to possessing this?
Would the original maker recognize what became of their work?
Is the name on this object still alive?

rare.description: Short questlines with 2-3 meaningful steps. Objects with obligations or contested histories.
hooks: The object responds differently depending on who holds it — it has opinions.
You have dreamed of this object three times before you ever found it.
Two different factions have sent representatives asking about it within the same week.
The object was listed as destroyed in an official record dated fifteen years ago.
Using it leaves a mark on you that others with knowledge recognize immediately.
It was part of a collection that was broken up under violent circumstances.
The object carries a minor curse — not enough to harm you, enough to remind you it is there.
A scholar offered you a small fortune for it without explaining why.
It is mentioned by name in a text you found somewhere completely unrelated.
The object feels different after certain events — as if it is paying attention.
Someone tried to steal it from you before you even knew what it was worth.
An old map was hidden inside it, folded tight and sealed with wax.
Three people have owned it in the past decade. None of them kept it long.
It was made as a weapon against something specific. That something may still exist.
The object is incomplete — a piece of it is missing, and you can feel the absence.
A dying person pressed it into your hands and said only 'finish what I started'.
It was sealed with a spell that kept it hidden for decades. Something broke the seal recently.
Every craftsperson who examines it says the same thing: this should not be possible to make.
origins: Forged as the centerpiece of a political alliance that collapsed into war.
Created by a mage who bound a fragment of their own soul into the making of it.
Belonged to a legendary figure whose legacy is now disputed — hero to some, monster to others.
Constructed by a secret order as a key to something they wanted kept locked.
Made as a gift between rivals who were briefly, unexpectedly, at peace.
Part of the payment for a contract that was never fulfilled.
Recovered from a dungeon that was sealed for a reason nobody recorded.
Smuggled across a border inside a shipment of mundane goods for twenty years.
Created during a magical experiment that went further than intended.
The last work of an artisan who was killed before they could explain what they had made.
Stolen from a temple vault three times and returned twice.
Built to contain something. Whether it still works is unclear.
Commissioned by a noble house that has since been erased from official histories.
Made in a tradition that was outlawed; the knowledge of how to make it should not exist.
Carried by a champion in a legendary contest. The contest had no honest winner.
steps_pools: Research the object's history — visit a library, a sage, or an old contact.
Discover the faction or individual with the strongest claim to it.
Decide whether to honor that claim, challenge it, or create a third option.
Face the consequences of your decision.
Find the missing piece or the person who has it.
Understand why it was separated in the first place.
Determine whether reuniting the pieces is safe — or wise.
Complete or abandon the object on your terms.
Follow the trail of previous owners backward through time.
Identify the moment the object became dangerous or contested.
Confront whatever is still unresolved from that moment.
Decide what story you want to add to the object's history.
Use the object as intended and observe what responds — person, creature, or place.
Follow that response to its source.
Negotiate, fight, or flee based on what you find.
Return changed.
Honor the dying person's request — find out what they started.
Discover why it was left unfinished.
Decide if you are the right person to finish it.
Finish it anyway.
reward_hints: The loyalty of an order that has been waiting for someone trustworthy.
Knowledge of a location that does not appear on any map.
A powerful enemy who now has reason to leave you alone.
The completed object — more powerful than any of its parts.
A name whispered in the right circles that opens unexpected doors.
A debt owed by someone who rarely owes anyone anything.
Answers to questions you did not know you had.
A title, informal but real, among people who matter.
oracle_seeds: Is there an active faction searching for this object with resources and intent?
Did the original owner die because of this object?
Is the object's power tied to something that still exists in the world?
Has this object been used to harm someone innocent?
Is there an obligation attached to ownership that the previous owner ignored?
Does possessing this object make you a target for something you cannot yet see?

very_rare.description: Multi-step arcs. The object is part of something larger already in motion.
hooks: The object was not lost. It was hidden — by someone who is still alive and still afraid.
Three separate people have died in the past year trying to reach where you found this.
An ancient compact names whoever holds this object as responsible for something enormous.
The object changes you in small ways each time you use it. So far the changes have been useful.
A prophecy you heard years ago mentioned something that matches this object exactly.
It was the prize in a conflict that technically never ended. Both sides still have soldiers.
The object has a name. Not a given name — its own name, in a language older than Common.
Something is drawn to it. You have noticed the same creature watching you from a distance three times.
Owning it means inheriting a war. You did not start it. That does not matter.
It was created to kill something specific. That thing is still alive.
The object is one of seven. You now have two. Someone has the other five.
Every person in your bloodline who held this died before their time. You are holding it now.
Using it at full power requires a price the previous owner refused to pay.
It was sealed inside a vault that required three different keys to open. You only had one.
The object is famous enough that your name will be attached to it in histories. How is up to you.
origins: Crafted at the convergence of a celestial event that occurs once every century.
The physical manifestation of a promise made between two powers that no longer speak.
Built as the anchor for a ward that protects an entire region — or used to.
Created by a civilization that destroyed itself and left no other record of its existence.
Shaped from a material that should not exist in this plane of reality.
The prize of a contest between gods, wagered casually and then forgotten.
Made to end a specific bloodline. The bloodline survived. The object was lost. Until now.
Forged with the willing sacrifice of the maker's future — they gave up what they would become.
The inheritance of an empire that fell so completely its name is not spoken anymore.
Built in pieces by seven different craftspeople who never met and never knew the whole design.
Created as the physical form of an oath sworn at the dawn of an age.
Stolen from the divine vault of a deity who noticed the theft.
Made to be the last resort of a hero who never needed it. Now someone does.
The result of a ritual meant to prevent something. The object is proof the ritual worked. Once.
steps_pools: Learn the object's true name and what it was made to do.
Find the others — people, objects, or places — connected to it.
Understand the full shape of what you have walked into.
Choose a side, or forge a third path no one anticipated.
See it through to an ending — yours or someone else's.
Locate the person who hid the object and find out what they feared.
Discover whether that fear was justified.
Face whatever they were hiding from.
Decide whether to finish what they started or let it remain buried.
Activate the object fully for the first time and observe the consequences.
Trace those consequences to their source.
Confront the original purpose the object was built for.
Choose whether to fulfill that purpose, subvert it, or destroy the object entirely.
Find the remaining pieces of the set this object belongs to.
Determine who has them and why they have not reunited the set themselves.
Unite the pieces — or prevent their reunion if that is the wiser path.
Reckon with what the completed set is capable of.
Follow the thread of deaths connected to this object back to the first one.
Identify what pattern ties them together.
Break the pattern — or become part of it knowingly.
Survive the ending you have chosen.
reward_hints: Mastery of the object's full power — whatever that costs.
The resolution of a conflict that has lasted longer than living memory.
Allies who are genuinely powerful and genuinely grateful.
A place in the histories — for better or worse.
The end of something that should have ended long ago.
Safety for a people, a place, or a principle that matters to you.
The right to walk away from something that would have consumed anyone else.
A truth that changes how you understand everything that came before.
oracle_seeds: Is there a living person whose fate is directly tied to this object?
Has the object been used at full power before — and what happened?
Is there a force — faction, creature, or power — that has been waiting for this object to resurface?
Does owning this object make you part of a prophecy whether you want to be or not?
Is the object's creator still alive, in any form?
What is the price of using this object as it was truly intended?

legendary.description: Full narrative arcs. The object is already shaping the world. Finding it was not an accident.
hooks: You were meant to find this. You do not know by whom. That is the first question.
The object has been waiting. Not dormant — waiting. There is a difference.
Three prophecies from three different traditions mention an object matching this description. None of them end well for the person who finds it.
Something ancient woke up the moment you touched it. You felt it happen.
The world is different now that you hold this. You cannot explain how you know.
It has chosen you. Not accepted you — chosen you. You will understand the distinction eventually.
Every major power in the known world knows this object exists. By tomorrow they will know you have it.
The object is not a reward. It is a test. You have already begun failing or passing — you do not know which.
There is a voice. It is not the object speaking. It is something speaking through the object.
The last person to hold this shaped the world. The world is still living with what they shaped.
You are the seventh. The previous six did not finish what they started.
Legends call this object by a name that means 'the beginning of the end'. Legends have been right before.
It was locked away by the most powerful alliance in recorded history. They could not destroy it. They settled for hiding it.
The object is incomplete. What it needs to be complete is you.
Finding this was the easy part. Everyone who found it thought that. They were right.
origins: Forged in the moment of a god's death, from the material of their passing.
The physical form of a concept — not a representation, the actual thing itself made solid.
Created at the end of an age as the only thing that could survive into the next one.
Built by the first civilization as a failsafe against a threat they knew would return.
The result of every mage in a generation pooling their power into a single act of creation.
Made from the willing transformation of a being who sacrificed their existence to become something useful.
Woven from the threads of fate itself by something that exists outside of time.
Constructed as the counterpart to something terrible — the only thing that can face it equally.
The inheritance of a lineage that predates recorded history, finally reaching its intended recipient.
Created in the space between worlds by forces that do not have names in any mortal language.
The last work of an entire tradition of magic that no longer exists — this object is the only proof it ever did.
Built to be found by the right person at the right time. Everything about your life has been leading here.
A wound in reality that was given shape and purpose rather than allowed to fester.
The answer to a question so large that the question itself was forgotten and only the answer remains.
steps_pools: Understand what you are holding — truly, not just its power but its purpose.
Find the others who are part of this story, willing or not.
Face the first real test: a moment where the easy path and the right path diverge completely.
Learn what the object costs — not in resources, but in who you are.
Confront the reason it exists: the thing it was made to face.
Choose what kind of ending you will write.
Follow the voice, the pull, the pattern — wherever it leads.
Discover who or what has been guiding you toward this moment.
Decide whether to trust that guidance or reject it.
Face the consequences of that decision at the worst possible time.
Find out if you were the person this was meant for — or if you became that person on the way.
Finish it. Whatever finishing it means.
Survive the first wave of those who want the object — they will come quickly.
Find an ally who understands what you are carrying.
Unlock the first layer of the object's true capability — and what it reveals about the world.
Face a choice that cannot be unmade.
Go to the place this object was made for.
Do what needs to be done.
Accept that your life before this was a preparation, not a story.
Gather what and who you need — time is not unlimited.
Breach whatever stands between you and the heart of this.
Pay the price that was always coming.
See what is on the other side.
Decide what you do with what you find there.
reward_hints: A changed world — smaller or larger, better or worse, but undeniably different.
The answer to why you. Finally, the answer to why you.
The end of a threat that has existed longer than anyone alive can remember.
A power that cannot be measured in game terms — something that simply is.
The freedom to stop. If you want it.
The knowledge that it mattered. That you mattered.
A new age. You get to decide what kind.
Whatever you were looking for when you started, even if you forgot you were looking.
oracle_seeds: Was finding this object truly random, or has something been arranging events for a long time?
Is there a living being whose entire existence is defined by this object's return?
What happens to the world if this object is destroyed rather than used?
Is the voice or pull coming from the object itself, or from something using the object as a channel?
Are you the person this was made for, or will you have to become them?
What is the name of the thing this object was created to face — and is it already aware you have it?
